cards.title: Magnet Therapy — Principles & Types

alerts.label: Definition
alerts.text: Magnet therapy (magnetotherapy) uses static or pulsed electromagnetic fields to influence biological processes including ion transport, cell membrane potential, enzyme activity, and circulation.

steps.title: North Pole (Negative)
steps.text: — Sedating, anti-inflammatory, pain-relieving, antimicrobial. Used for acute pain, inflammation, infection, tumours, insomnia. Apply NORTH pole to painful/inflamed area.

steps.title: South Pole (Positive)
steps.text: — Stimulating, promotes growth and circulation. Used for chronic weakness, paralysis, chronic pain, hypothyroidism. Use with CAUTION — can accelerate abnormal cell growth.

steps.title: Bipolar (Both poles)
steps.text: — General tonic effect. Used for systemic conditions, metabolic disorders. Smaller magnets alternating poles for broad application.

cards._legacyHtml:
Definition
Magnet therapy (magnetotherapy) uses static or pulsed electromagnetic fields to influence biological processes including ion transport, cell membrane potential, enzyme activity, and circulation.
TypeFieldStrengthPenetrationUse
Static magnetsPermanent300–5000 GaussSuperficial 2–3cmPain, local conditions
ElectromagnetsPulsed (PEMF)VariableDeep tissueBone healing, systemic
Magnetic waterConditioned1000–3000 GaussSystemic (oral)Renal stones, HTN, digestion
Magnetic insolesPermanent800–1200 GaussFoot reflexologyFatigue, peripheral circulation
N
North Pole (Negative) — Sedating, anti-inflammatory, pain-relieving, antimicrobial. Used for acute pain, inflammation, infection, tumours, insomnia. Apply NORTH pole to painful/inflamed area.
S
South Pole (Positive) — Stimulating, promotes growth and circulation. Used for chronic weakness, paralysis, chronic pain, hypothyroidism. Use with CAUTION — can accelerate abnormal cell growth.
B
Bipolar (Both poles) — General tonic effect. Used for systemic conditions, metabolic disorders. Smaller magnets alternating poles for broad application.

cards.title: Magnet Therapy — Indications & Protocols

pearls.label: 🟡 Pearl
pearls.text: Magnetic water preparation: fill a glass bottle with clean water, place on a magnet (1000–3000G) for 12–24 hours. North-pole water is sedating/anti-inflammatory; south-pole water is stimulating. Most clinical use is north-pole or bipolar water. Magnetic water has shown benefit in kidney stone dissolution and HTN in small trials.

MigraineNorthTemporal/occipital20 min onsetApply at headache onset
🟡 Pearl
Magnetic water preparation: fill a glass bottle with clean water, place on a magnet (1000–3000G) for 12–24 hours. North-pole water is sedating/anti-inflammatory; south-pole water is stimulating. Most clinical use is north-pole or bipolar water. Magnetic water has shown benefit in kidney stone dissolution and HTN in small trials.

cards.title: Magnet Therapy — Contraindications

alerts.label: Absolute Contraindications ★
alerts.text: Pacemakers or implanted cardiac devices · Pregnancy (especially first trimester) · Active bleeding / haemophilia · Open wounds or recent surgery · Implanted metal prosthetics at treatment site · Epilepsy (PEMF) · Myasthenia gravis.

rows: Avoid PEMF; low gauss static with caution
South pole may stimulate cell growth
Use North pole only if treating pain in cancer

cards._legacyHtml:
Absolute Contraindications ★
Pacemakers or implanted cardiac devices · Pregnancy (especially first trimester) · Active bleeding / haemophilia · Open wounds or recent surgery · Implanted metal prosthetics at treatment site · Epilepsy (PEMF) · Myasthenia gravis.
ContraindicationReasonAction
Pacemaker ★Magnetic field disrupts device timingABSOLUTE — never apply any magnet
PregnancyUnknown fetal effectsAvoid PEMF; low gauss static with caution
Cancer (South pole)South pole may stimulate cell growthUse North pole only if treating pain in cancer
FeverAlready vasodilated — south pole worsensUse North pole only
HypotensionNorth pole reduces BP furtherUse with monitoring

cards.title: PEMF Therapy — Evidence-Based Applications

alerts.label: PEMF — FDA Approved Applications
alerts.text: FDA has approved PEMF devices for: non-union bone fractures (1979), post-surgical pain and oedema (2004), major depression (2011 — TMS), cervical fusion (2009). These represent the strongest evidence base.

pearls.label: 🟡 PEMF vs Static Magnets
pearls.text: PEMF penetrates the entire body; static magnets only reach 2–3cm. PEMF induces small electric currents via Faraday induction; static magnets do not. Mechanism of PEMF: (1) Ca2+ influx via VGCC → calmodulin → nitric oxide synthesis. (2) ATP production enhancement. (3) Normalises resting membrane potential in damaged cells. (4) Upregulates TGF-b, VEGF, FGF. Key advantage: PEMF acts at cellular level regardless of tissue depth — no current limitation. Consumer PEMF mats (low intensity) have moderate evidence; clinical devices (higher intensity) have stronger evidence.

cards._legacyHtml:
PEMF — FDA Approved Applications
FDA has approved PEMF devices for: non-union bone fractures (1979), post-surgical pain and oedema (2004), major depression (2011 — TMS), cervical fusion (2009). These represent the strongest evidence base.
ConditionEvidenceParametersSessions
Non-union fracturesFDA approved — 70–80% union rate72Hz, 1–2mT, 8–10 hrs/day3–6 months
OsteoarthritisStrong — reduces VAS pain 40%17Hz, 0.1–1mT, 30 min BD4–6 weeks
OsteoporosisGood — increases bone density75Hz, 0.1mT, 30 min/day6–12 months
Chronic painGood — reduces opioid requirement50Hz, 1mT, 30 min OD8 weeks
Wound healingGood — accelerates healing 30%Low freq PEMF or 27.12MHz SWDDaily × 4 weeks
FibromyalgiaModerate — pain and sleep benefit200Hz burst, 1mT, 30 min BD4–8 weeks
MS fatigueModerate4Hz, 0.025mT, 8 min/day4 weeks
🟡 PEMF vs Static Magnets
PEMF penetrates the entire body; static magnets only reach 2–3cm. PEMF induces small electric currents via Faraday induction; static magnets do not. Mechanism of PEMF: (1) Ca2+ influx via VGCC → calmodulin → nitric oxide synthesis. (2) ATP production enhancement. (3) Normalises resting membrane potential in damaged cells. (4) Upregulates TGF-b, VEGF, FGF. Key advantage: PEMF acts at cellular level regardless of tissue depth — no current limitation. Consumer PEMF mats (low intensity) have moderate evidence; clinical devices (higher intensity) have stronger evidence.
